name: Fear and Shock Tests
summaryText: Failing a Courage-based Fear Test adds +10 per Degree of Failure to the Shock Test result.

fearExamples.label: An ambush from a superior force
An ally takes half of their Wounds in a single Attack
Witnessing the Flood for the first time

sourceText: When making a Fear Test, the Character must roll a Courage Test. The GM decides on the Modifiers, such as something being Disturbing, and add the Fear Modifiers to the Courage Test. If the Character fails, they must count their Degrees of Failure. For every Degree of Failure, the Character adds +10 to figuring the outcome of the Shock Test.

name: Shock Test
summaryText: Shock outcomes range from being startled to PTSD, amnesia, and Courage damage.

outcomes.effect: Only badly startled; may only take a single Half Action until the beginning of next turn.
Gripped by fear; all tests at -10 until passing a Courage Test, +10 to each subsequent test if the previous failed.
Deeply disturbed; cannot willingly approach the source unless passing a Courage Test, +10 to each subsequent test if the previous failed.
Nearly freezes; must make a Courage Test or stay frozen, +10 to each subsequent test if the previous failed.
Frozen from fear for 1D5 - Toughness Modifier Turns, minimum 1.
Develops a PTSD effect and takes 1D10 Courage damage that takes 2D10 - Courage Modifier days to recover, minimum 2.
Falls into short amnesia for 5D10 - Toughness Modifier days, minimum 5; upon waking, takes 1D10 Courage damage and develops a PTSD effect.

name: PTSD
summaryText: PTSD manifests as Phobias, Obsessions, Nightmares, Delusions, or Tantrums.
sourceText: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, often known as PTSD for short, is a series of mental disorders that develop from experiencing traumatic events. When a Character gains PTSD, it will manifest in various ways.

name: Phobias
summaryText: Phobias impose a -20 penalty to future Fear Tests dealing with the listed fear.

entries.name: Aerophobia
entries.description: The fear of flying.

entries.name: Agoraphobia
entries.description: The fear of vast openness.

entries.name: Astrophobia
entries.description: The fear of outer space.

entries.name: Autophobia
entries.description: The fear of being alone.

entries.name: Claustrophobia
entries.description: The fear of confined spaces.

entries.name: Entomophobia
entries.description: The fear of insects.

entries.name: Eremophobia
entries.description: The fear that allies may turn on you.

entries.name: Hemophobia
entries.description: The fear of blood.

entries.name: Hoplophobia
entries.description: The fear of weapons and using them.

entries.name: Hypnophobia
entries.description: The fear of sleep, especially cryosleep.

entries.name: Necrophobia
entries.description: The fear of death and dead bodies.

entries.name: Nyctophobia
entries.description: The fear of darkness.

entries.name: Panthophobia
entries.description: The fear of becoming sick and diseased.

entries.name: Taphophobia
entries.description: The fear of moving fast.

entries.name: Xenophobia
entries.description: The fear of aliens and strangers.

name: Obsessions and Manias
summaryText: Examples of obsessive or manic behaviors chosen by GM and players.

entries.name: Agromania
entries.description: The obsession to be in open spaces.

entries.name: Arithmomania
entries.description: A manic tendency of tracking numbers and counting objects and occurrences.

entries.name: Claustromania
entries.description: An irrational need to be in enclosed spaces.

entries.name: Egomania
entries.description: Irrational self-centered attitude.

entries.name: Eleutheromania
entries.description: The obsession of being free from military or any other company or organization.

entries.name: Ergomania
entries.description: Irrational need to always be working.

entries.name: Eudemonia
entries.description: An obsession with wandering and traveling.

entries.name: Kleptomania
entries.description: The obsession of stealing objects from others.

entries.name: Logomania
entries.description: The obsession to always be talking.

entries.name: Ludomania
entries.description: An obsession with gambling.

entries.name: Lypemania
entries.description: The manic tendency for never seeing anything as good or welcoming.

entries.name: Megalomania
entries.description: An obsession for gaining more power.

entries.name: Mythomania
entries.description: The manic tendency to tell lies.

entries.name: Oniomania
entries.description: The obsession of spending cR.

entries.name: Plutomania
entries.description: An obsession with collecting or hoarding cR.

entries.name: Polemanias
entries.description: The obsession of war and violence.

entries.name: Technomania
entries.description: An obsession for technology.

entries.name: Theomania
entries.description: An obsession of being on a divine mission.

entries.name: Xenomania
entries.description: An inordinate obsession with aliens.

name: Nightmares
summaryText: Each night, make a Courage Test; on failure, make a Toughness Test at -10 or begin gaining Fatigue over time, up to 2 Fatigue total.
sourceText: A character that has recurring nightmares must roll a Courage Test every night when asleep. If the character fails this Courage Test, a Toughness Test must be made with a -10 Penalty, if failed, the character begins to gain fatigue over time for not having good night sleep. Only two fatigue can be gained in total from having Nightmares.

name: Tantrums
summaryText: -30 to Social Skill Tests during a Tantrum and -10 to all Actions.
mechanicalText: A Tantrum can be overcome through roleplay or Social Skill Tests at -20 for the person attempting to calm them.
sourceText: Characters who are prone to Tantrums will gain a -30 Penalty to Social Skill Tests during a Tantrum and all Actions made are at a -10 Penalty. A Tantrum can be overcome by being talked down with roleplaying or through Social Skill Tests with a -20 Penalty for the person attempting to calm them.

name: Delusions and Hallucinations
summaryText: Delusions and hallucinations follow the same principles as phobias and manias.

entries.name: Auditory
entries.description: False perception of voices and sounds.

entries.name: Broadcast
entries.description: A delusion of one's thoughts being read by others.

entries.name: Control
entries.description: The false belief that another person, group, or external force controls one's thoughts, feelings, impulses, or behaviors.

entries.name: Erotomania
entries.description: The false belief that others are in love with or obsessed with one's self.

entries.name: Grandiose
entries.description: The delusion that one is magic or untouchable through luck.

entries.name: Guilt
entries.description: An ungrounded feeling of constant remorse or guilt for events outside of their control.

entries.name: Gustatory
entries.description: The false perception of tastes.

entries.name: Hypnogogic
entries.description: Events before waking that cannot be differentiated from reality.

entries.name: Olfactory
entries.description: The false perception of smells and scents.

entries.name: Paranoid
entries.description: The belief that one is being controlled or persecuted by stealth powers and conspiracies.

entries.name: Persecutory
entries.description: A belief that one is being persecuted for any action being made.

entries.name: Poverty
entries.description: A strong belief that they are financially incapacitated regardless of wealth.

entries.name: Referential
entries.description: The delusion that events carry hidden or coded messages.

entries.name: Sensation
entries.description: Hallucinations that affect sensations triggered by events.

entries.name: Somatic
entries.description: The false perception of processes and events happening inside the body.

entries.name: Tactile
entries.description: False sensations of being touched, crawled upon, or feeling processes under the skin.

entries.name: Thought Insertion
entries.description: The false belief that outside or unknown internal forces are injecting thoughts into one's mind.

entries.name: Visual
entries.description: The false perception of objects, people, and events happening before one's eyes.
